message:
test(sync): add fuzz crash regression suite and expand merge_issue corpus

Adds tests/repro_sync_cycle_fuzz_crashes.rs — 13 regression tests that
replay minimized sync_cycle fuzz crash inputs through the real sync
infrastructure (storage, export_to_jsonl, import_from_jsonl, preflight
checks, conflict-marker detection, staleness/hash computation). Each test
is labeled with its crash artifact ID and asserts the input no longer
panics after the c0f7749 hardening commit, giving us a cheap way to catch
any future regression that reintroduces one of the original bugs.

Coverage highlights:
- Conflict-marker rejection (plain and with embedded content strings)
- Corrupt / malformed JSONL rejected by import without panics
- Staleness and content-hash helpers on degenerate inputs
- Export/import round-trips over adversarial issue IDs, titles, and
  comment payloads discovered by the fuzzer
- Orphan-mode selection and preflight_import on pathological workspaces

Also seeds fuzz/corpus/merge_issue/ with 790 new coverage-discovered
inputs accumulated by running the merge_issue libfuzzer target against
the newly hardened code paths. Committing these pays down the cost of
rediscovering them on every fresh fuzz run and lets CI/cron fuzzers hit
interesting states immediately. All files are individual binary blobs
managed by cargo-fuzz; none require rust source changes.

Why commit corpus files:
- Precedent: 8cbc1a6 and related earlier commits already track the
  coverage-discovered merge_issue and sync_cycle seeds; this commit keeps
  that policy consistent.
- Fast cold-start for agents and CI: new fuzz runs begin from a rich
  corpus instead of having to rediscover structure via mutations.
